content.title: Movement with 回 (return patterns)
content.body:
回 (huí) expresses returning to an origin point. It commonly pairs with:
- **回家** (huí jiā) – return home / go home
- **回 + country** – return to one's home country
- **回 + origin location** – return to where you're from

Context matters:
- 我**回**中国 (wǒ **huí** Zhōngguó) – I return to China (implies

content.title: 来 (lái) – come
content.body:
The verb 来 (lái) means "to come" – movement toward the speaker or toward a reference point.

Basic structure:
- 我**来** (wǒ **lái**) – I come
- 你**来** (nǐ **lái**) – you come
- 他**来** (tā **lái**) – he comes

来 indicates motion toward "here" from the speaker's perspective.

Examples:
- 你来！ (nǐ lái!)

content.title: Movement narrative comprehension
content.body:
我今天去学校。我来学校，他去家。他回家了。

(wǒ jīntiān qù xuéxiào. wǒ lái xuéxiào, tā qù jiā. tā huí jiā le.)

Vocabulary:
- 今天 (jīntiān) – today
- 了 (le) – completed action marker

content.title: Directional complement: verb + 来
content.body:
A directional complement adds direction information to a verb. **Verb + 来** indicates movement toward the speaker.

Common patterns:
- **进来** (jìn lái) – come in (enter toward speaker)
- **出来** (chū lái) – come out (exit toward speaker)
- **上来** (shàng lái) – come up (ascend toward speaker)
- **下来**

content.title: 来 + place (destination structure)
content.body:
To express "come to [place]", use: **来 + place**

Structure: Subject + 来 + Place

Examples:
- 你**来我家** (nǐ **lái wǒ jiā**) – You come to my home
- 他**来学校** (tā **lái xuéxiào**) – He comes to school
- 她**来中国** (tā **lái Zhōngguó**) – She comes to China

Like 去, no particle is needed between 来 and the

content.title: 去 + place (destination structure)
content.body:
To express "go to [place]", use: **去 + place**

Structure: Subject + 去 + Place

Examples:
- 我**去学校** (wǒ **qù xuéxiào**) – I go to school
- 他**去医院** (tā **qù yīyuàn**) – He goes to the hospital
- 你**去图书馆** (nǐ **qù túshūguǎn**) – You go to the library

No additional particle is needed between 去 and

content.title: 进 (jìn) – enter
content.body:
The verb 进 (jìn) means "to enter" or "to go in" – movement into a space.

Basic structure:
- 我**进** (wǒ **jìn**) – I enter
- 你**进来** (nǐ **jìn lái**) – you come in
- 他**进去** (tā **jìn qù**) – he goes in

Examples:
- 请进！ (qǐng jìn!) – Please come in!
- 我进 (wǒ jìn) – I go in / I enter
- 他进学校 (tā jìn x

content.title: 去 (qù) – go
content.body:
The verb 去 (qù) means "to go" – movement away from the speaker or away from a reference point.

Basic structure:
- 我**去** (wǒ **qù**) – I go
- 你**去** (nǐ **qù**) – you go
- 他**去** (tā **qù**) – he goes

去 indicates motion away from "here" from the speaker's perspective.

Examples:
- 我去 (wǒ qù) – I'm

content.title: 下 (xià) – go down
content.body:
The verb 下 (xià) means "to go down", "to descend", or "to get off" – downward movement or dismounting.

Basic uses:
- 下 can mean physically going down
- 下 can mean getting off (a vehicle)

Examples:
- 我**下** (wǒ **xià**) – I go down
- 下来 (xià lái) – come down
- 下去 (xià qù) – go down

content.title: 回 (huí) – return
content.body:
The verb 回 (huí) means "to return" or "to go back" – movement back to an origin point.

Basic structure:
- 我**回** (wǒ **huí**) – I return
- 你**回家** (nǐ **huí jiā**) – you return home
- 他**回** (tā **huí**) – he returns

回 is commonly used with 家 (home): 回家 (huí jiā) – return home / go home

Examples:

content.title: 出 (chū) – exit
content.body:
The verb 出 (chū) means "to exit" or "to go out" – movement out of a space.

Basic structure:
- 我**出** (wǒ **chū**) – I exit / I go out
- 你**出来** (nǐ **chū lái**) – you come out
- 他**出去** (tā **chū qù**) – he goes out

Examples:
- 我出去 (wǒ chū qù) – I go out
- 他出来了 (tā chū lái le) – He came out
- 出！ (

content.title: Directional complements with location context
content.body:
The choice between 来 and 去 in directional complements depends on speaker position and perspective.

Scenario: Speaker is outside a room.
- 他**进去**了 (tā **jìn qù** le) – He went in (away from speaker, into room)
- 你**出来**！ (nǐ **chū lái**!) – Come out! (toward speaker, from room)

Scenario: Speaker i

content.title: Giving directions with movement verbs
content.body:
Movement verbs combine to give clear directions about where to go and how to enter/exit.

Example directions:
- 你去学校，进去，上楼 (nǐ qù xuéxiào, jìn qù, shàng lóu) – You go to school, go in, go upstairs
- 请来我家，进来，上来 (qǐng lái wǒ jiā, jìn lái, shàng lái) – Please come to my home, come in, come up

Notice h

content.title: Dialogue: Planning movement
content.body:
A: 你去哪儿？ (nǐ qù nǎr?) – Where are you going?

B: 我去学校。你来吗？ (wǒ qù xuéxiào. nǐ lái ma?) – I'm going to school. Are you coming?

A: 我不去。我回家。 (wǒ bù qù. wǒ huí jiā.) – I'm not going. I'm going home.

B: 好。 (hǎo.) – Okay.

Vocabulary:
- 哪儿 (nǎr) – where
- 吗 (ma) – question particle
- 不 (bù) – not
- 好 (h

content.title: Vertical movement: 上 and 下 in context
content.body:
上 (shàng) and 下 (xià) describe vertical movement and also boarding/exiting vehicles.

Physical vertical movement:
- 上楼 (shàng lóu) – go upstairs
- 下楼 (xià lóu) – go downstairs

With directional complements:
- 上来 (shàng lái) – come up
- 上去 (shàng qù) – go up
- 下来 (xià lái) – come down
- 下去 (xià qù) –

content.title: Directional complement: verb + 去
content.body:
A directional complement with **verb + 去** indicates movement away from the speaker.

Common patterns:
- **进去** (jìn qù) – go in (enter away from speaker)
- **出去** (chū qù) – go out (exit away from speaker)
- **上去** (shàng qù) – go up (ascend away from speaker)
- **下去** (xià qù) – go down (descend a

content.title: Contrast: 来 vs 去 with destinations
content.body:
When using destinations with 来 and 去, choose based on the speaker's perspective:

**去 + place** = going away from current location to that place
**来 + place** = coming toward current location / reference point

Examples from speaker at home:
- 我**去**学校 (wǒ **qù** xuéxiào) – I go to school (leaving h

content.title: 上 (shàng) – go up
content.body:
The verb 上 (shàng) means "to go up", "to ascend", or "to get on" – upward movement or mounting.

Basic uses:
- 上 can mean physically going up
- 上 can mean getting on (a vehicle)

Examples:
- 我**上** (wǒ **shàng**) – I go up
- 上来 (shàng lái) – come up
- 上去 (shàng qù) – go up

content.title: Combining movement verbs in context
content.body:
Movement verbs often appear together in natural conversation to describe sequences or contrasts.

Example dialogue:
- A: 你去哪儿？ (nǐ qù nǎr?) – Where are you going?
- B: 我去学校 (wǒ qù xuéxiào) – I'm going to school

Example narrative:
- 他来我家，我去他家 (tā lái wǒ jiā, wǒ qù tā jiā) – He comes to my home, I go

content.title: Polite invitation with 请 + movement verb
content.body:
请 (qǐng) + movement verb creates polite invitations or requests.

Common patterns:
- **请进** (qǐng jìn) – Please come in
- **请来** (qǐng lái) – Please come
- **请进来** (qǐng jìn lái) – Please come in (more specific direction)

These are standard hospitality phrases in Chinese.

Example context:
- Someon
